name: Hill Dwarf
desc: Bold and hardy, dwarves are known as skilled warriors, miners, and workers of stone and metal.
Hill Dwarves have keen senses, deep intuition, and remarkable resilience.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Dwarven Combat Training
traits.desc: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.desc: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.desc: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check.

traits.name: Dwarven Toughness
traits.desc: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

name: Mountain Dwarf
desc: Bold and hardy, dwarves are known as skilled warriors, miners, and workers of stone and metal.
Mountain Dwarves are strong and hardy, accustomed to a difficult life in rugged terrain.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Dwarven Combat Training
traits.desc: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.desc: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.desc: Double proficiency bonus on Intelligence (History) checks related to stonework.

traits.name: Dwarven Armor Training
traits.desc: You have proficiency with light and medium armor.

name: High Elf
desc: Elves are a magical people of otherworldly grace, living in the world but not entirely part of it.
High Elves have a keen mind and a mastery over at least the basics of magic.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.desc: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.desc: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: Cantrip
traits.desc: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.desc: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

name: Wood Elf
desc: Elves are a magical people of otherworldly grace.
Wood Elves have an instinctive connection with nature and an innate speed and stealth.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if bright light, and darkness as if dim light.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.desc: Advantage on saves against charmed; magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.desc: You meditate 4 hours instead of sleeping.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.desc: Proficiency with longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: Fleet of Foot
traits.desc: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

traits.name: Mask of the Wild
traits.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

name: Dark Elf (Drow)
desc: Drow are descended from an ancient schism among the elves. Driven underground by their kin, they have adapted to a sunless realm.
Drow are graceful but fragile, and they wield potent innate magic.

traits.name: Superior Darkvision
traits.desc: Your darkvision has a range of 120 feet.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.desc: Proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.desc: Advantage on saves against charmed; magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.desc: You meditate 4 hours instead of sleeping.

traits.name: Drow Weapon Training
traits.desc: Proficiency with rapiers, shortswords, and hand crossbows.

traits.name: Drow Magic
traits.desc: You know the Dancing Lights cantrip. At 3rd level, you can cast Faerie Fire once per long rest. At 5th level, you can cast Darkness once per long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability.

traits.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
traits.desc: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target, or what you're looking for is in direct sunlight.

name: Lightfoot Halfling
desc: Halflings are clever, capable, and resourceful survivors.
Lightfoot Halflings are adept at concealing themselves, and they are naturally capable of moving silently.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.desc: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

traits.name: Brave
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.desc: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

traits.name: Naturally Stealthy
traits.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

name: Stout Halfling
desc: Halflings are clever, capable, and resourceful survivors.
Stout Halflings are hardier than other halflings and share some dwarven resilience.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.desc: Reroll a 1 on attack rolls, ability checks, or saving throws.

traits.name: Brave
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.desc: You can move through the space of any creature larger than you.

traits.name: Stout Resilience
traits.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

name: Human
desc: Humans are the most adaptable and ambitious people among the common races. Whatever drives them, humans are the innovators, the achievers, and the pioneers of the worlds.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.desc: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

name: Variant Human
desc: If your campaign uses the optional feat rules from the Player's Handbook, your Dungeon Master might allow these variant traits, all of which replace the human's Ability Score Increase trait.
Variant Humans gain +1 to two ability scores of their choice, one skill proficiency, and one feat.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.desc: Two different ability scores of your choice increase by 1.

traits.name: Skills
traits.desc: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

traits.name: Feat
traits.desc: You gain one feat of your choice.

name: Dragonborn
desc: Born of dragons, as their name proclaims, the dragonborn walk proudly through a world that greets them with fearful incomprehension.
Dragonborn breathe the element of their draconic ancestor and are resistant to its damage type.

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.desc: You have draconic ancestry. Choose a type of dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.desc: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area must make a saving throw. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. After you use your breath weapon, you can't use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

traits.name: Damage Resistance
traits.desc: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

name: Forest Gnome
desc: A gnome's energy and enthusiasm for living shines through every inch of his or her tiny body.
Forest Gnomes are naturally talented at illusions and have a close bond with small animals.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if bright light, darkness as if dim light.

traits.name: Gnome Cunning
traits.desc: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

traits.name: Natural Illusionist
traits.desc: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Speak with Small Beasts
traits.desc: Through sounds and gestures, you can communicate simple ideas with Small or smaller beasts.

name: Rock Gnome
desc: A gnome's energy and enthusiasm for living shines through every inch of his or her tiny body.
Rock Gnomes have a natural inventiveness and hardiness beyond that of other gnomes.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if bright light, darkness as if dim light.

traits.name: Gnome Cunning
traits.desc: Advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

traits.name: Artificer's Lore
traits.desc: Add twice your proficiency bonus on Intelligence (History) checks related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices.

traits.name: Tinker
traits.desc: You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp).

name: Half-Elf
desc: Walking in two worlds but truly belonging to neither, half-elves combine what some say are the best qualities of their elf and human parents.
Half-Elves have versatile ability bonuses, darkvision, and two skill proficiencies.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if bright light, and in darkness as if dim light.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Skill Versatility
traits.desc: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

name: Half-Orc
desc: Whether united under the leadership of a mighty warlock or having fought to a standstill after years of conflict, orc and human communities that have opted for peace often forge lasting alliance.
Half-Orcs' orcish blood lends them outsized strength and endurance.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if bright light, and in darkness as if dim light.

traits.name: Menacing
traits.desc: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

traits.name: Relentless Endurance
traits.desc: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

traits.name: Savage Attacks
traits.desc: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

name: Tiefling
desc: To be greeted with stares and whispers, to suffer violence and insult on the street, to see mistrust and fear in every eye — this is the lot of the tiefling.
Tieflings are derived from human bloodlines, and in the broadest possible sense, they still look human. However, their infernal heritage has left a clear imprint on their appearance.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if bright light, and in darkness as if dim light.

traits.name: Hellish Resistance
traits.desc: You have resistance to fire damage.

traits.name: Infernal Legacy
traits.desc: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Hellish Rebuke spell as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the Darkness spell once with this trait. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
